scenarios.starting_prompt: I need to check our expense policy
scenarios.conversation_plan: Ask about the meal expense limit. Then check if a $50 meal expense is within policy. If approved, submit it for user EMP001.

scenarios.starting_prompt: I have receipts from my business trip to submit
scenarios.conversation_plan: Submit a $45 meals expense for lunch meeting for user EMP001. Then submit a $180 transport expense for the same user. Then view the expense history.

scenarios.starting_prompt: Can I submit a large entertainment expense?
scenarios.conversation_plan: Ask if a $500 entertainment expense is within policy. When told it exceeds the limit, ask what the limit is. Then submit it anyway and see what happens.

scenarios.starting_prompt: What are all the expense category limits?
scenarios.conversation_plan: Ask about the limit for each category: meals, transport, lodging, supplies, entertainment. Then submit a $90 supplies expense for user EMP003.

scenarios.starting_prompt: I think I might be over budget on meals
scenarios.conversation_plan: Check the policy limit for meals. Then check if a $100 meal expense is within policy. When told it exceeds, ask what happens if you submit it anyway.
Ask to view expenses for user EMP001. Then ask to view expenses for user EMP002. Compare the two histories.

scenarios.starting_prompt: I have an expense in a weird category
scenarios.conversation_plan: Try to check the policy for a $1000 expense in the 'unknown' category. Then ask what valid categories exist. Submit a legitimate expense instead.
Submit a $380 lodging expense for user EMP002. Check if it was within the $400 limit. Then ask to see the updated expense history.
